steps.title: Fastest path: compare perimeters first
steps.content: For similar triangles, corresponding sides use the same scale factor as their perimeters. Once the perimeter ratio is known, multiply the matching side length by that ratio.

steps.title: Use the perimeter ratio to find the scale factor
steps.content: For similar triangles, corresponding sides are proportional to their perimeters. Find the scale factor: $$k = \frac{\text{Perimeter of } XYZ}{\text{Perimeter of } TUV} = \frac{160}{40} = 4$$

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to the corresponding side
steps.content: Since $\overline{TU}$ corresponds to $\overline{XY}$ and the scale factor is 4: $$XY = TU \times 4 = 8 \times 4 = 32$$ That confirms C is correct.
